description:
Dynamic-dispatch boundaries route through the `any`-key specialization. At these sites the static caller is opaque, so we can't pick a specialization based on arg Descrs — fall back to the conservative any-key body, which behaves exactly as today's single-FnTypes-per-fn compilation does.

## Boundaries this covers

- `Prim::MakeClosure` — the closure value carries no static caller binding.
- `Term::CallClosure` / `Term::TailCallClosure` — dispatch is via fn-ptr at runtime.
- `Term::Receive` / fz_send — message arrives deep-copied, no static type.
- Anything else where the caller is statically opaque.

## What changes

- ir_typer's `closure_reachable` skip becomes "use any-key for this fn" instead of "skip narrowing entirely." Removes one workaround.
- The any-key specialization of every closure-reachable fn is unconditionally instantiated (it's the fallback target).
- Direct-call sites continue to use narrowed specializations from .29.2.

## What stays

- Closure heap payload kind plumbing (still tagged FzValue at the boundary by default).
- The runtime closure-invoke path's blind 8-byte copy works because the any-key callee's slots are FzValue.

## Acceptance

- Closure / Spawn / Receive paths use the any-key specialization, not skipped narrowing.
- `closure_reachable` set still computed (used to decide which fns need an any-key emitted as fallback) but no longer drives a `continue` in the typer.
- Fixture matrix green; closure fixtures unchanged in CLIF.
- Spawn / message-passing fixtures unchanged in CLIF.

## Blocked by

fz-ul4.29.2.
title: .29.3: Closure/Spawn/Receive any-key fallback
